panels.title: Completed Runs
panels.description: How many bot runs finished in the selected time window. A sudden drop usually means runs are failing or stalling before completion.

targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_runs WHERE $__timeFilter(COALESCE(ended_at, started_at)) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${status}' = '__all' OR status = '${status}') AND status = 'completed';

panels.title: Run Duration p50 / p95 (seconds)
panels.description: Median and p95 full-run duration. If p95 keeps climbing while p50 is flat, only some runs are degrading; if both climb, the whole system is slowing down.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'p50' AS metric, percentile_cont(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ended_at - started_at)))::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_runs WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ended_at IS NOT NULL AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${status}' = '__all' OR status = '${status}') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'p95' AS metric, percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ended_at - started_at)))::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_runs WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ended_at IS NOT NULL AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${status}' = '__all' OR status = '${status}') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Step Duration p95 by Step (ms)
panels.description: Shows p95 latency for each step. A step line that rises over time is your likely drift source.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), step_name AS metric, percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') GROUP BY 1, step_name ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Selected Step Avg / p95 (ms)
panels.description: Zoom in on one step to confirm gradual slowdown. If both average and p95 trend up, the step itself is drifting, not just outliers.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'avg' AS metric, avg(duration_ms)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${step_name}' = '__all' OR step_name = '${step_name}') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'p95' AS metric, percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${step_name}' = '__all' OR step_name = '${step_name}') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Step Throughput (events/min)
panels.description: How many step traces are recorded per minute. If throughput falls while runtime rises, work is backing up or waiting on I/O.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), step_name AS metric, (count(*)::double precision * 60.0) / GREATEST($__interval_ms / 1000.0, 1.0) AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') GROUP BY 1, step_name ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Step Error Rate (%)
panels.description: Percent of step traces marked failed. Anything consistently above 1% is worth immediate investigation.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), step_name AS metric, 100.0 * avg(CASE WHEN ok THEN 0.0 ELSE 1.0 END)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') GROUP BY 1, step_name ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Step Drift Detector (Last vs Previous Window)
panels.description: Compares recent average step time to the previous equal window. Ratios above 1.5x indicate real drift, above 2.0x is severe.

targets.rawSql: WITH bounds AS ( SELECT $__timeFrom()::timestamptz AS t_from, $__timeTo()::timestamptz AS t_to ), win AS ( SELECT (t_to - t_from) AS span, t_to, t_from, t_from - (t_to - t_from) AS prev_from FROM bounds ), recent AS ( SELECT step_name, avg(duration_ms)::double precision AS avg_ms FROM portal_bot_run_steps, win WHERE started_at >= win.t_from AND started_at <= win.t_to AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') GROUP BY step_name ), previous AS ( SELECT step_name, avg(duration_ms)::double precision AS avg_ms FROM portal_bot_run_steps, win WHERE started_at >= win.prev_from AND started_at < win.t_from AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') GROUP BY step_name ) SELECT r.step_name, ROUND(r.avg_ms::numeric, 2)::double precision AS recent_avg_ms, CASE WHEN p.avg_ms IS NULL THEN NULL ELSE ROUND(p.avg_ms::numeric, 2)::double precision END AS previous_avg_ms, ROUND((r.avg_ms / NULLIF(p.avg_ms, 0))::numeric, 2)::double precision AS drift_ratio FROM recent r LEFT JOIN previous p ON p.step_name = r.step_name ORDER BY drift_ratio DESC NULLS LAST, recent_avg_ms DESC LIMIT 25;

panels.title: Slowest Runs
panels.description: Longest runs with step volume and trade counts. Use this to find outlier runs, then drill into that run_id.

targets.rawSql: SELECT r.run_id, r.bot_id, r.status, ROUND(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (r.ended_at - r.started_at))::numeric, 2)::double precision AS run_seconds, COALESCE(s.step_events, 0) AS step_events, COALESCE(t.trade_count, 0) AS trades, r.started_at FROM portal_bot_runs r LEFT JOIN ( SELECT run_id, count(*) AS step_events FROM portal_bot_run_steps GROUP BY run_id ) s ON s.run_id = r.run_id LEFT JOIN ( SELECT run_id, count(*) AS trade_count FROM portal_bot_trades GROUP BY run_id ) t ON t.run_id = r.run_id WHERE $__timeFilter(r.started_at) AND r.ended_at IS NOT NULL AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR r.bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${status}' = '__all' OR r.status = '${status}') ORDER BY run_seconds DESC LIMIT 50;

panels.title: Slowest Steps in Selected Run
panels.description: Breaks down one run by step latency. If one step dominates total time, optimize that first.

targets.rawSql: SELECT step_name, count(*) AS calls, ROUND(avg(duration_ms)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS avg_ms, ROUND(percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS p95_ms, ROUND(max(duration_ms)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS max_ms, ROUND((sum(duration_ms) / NULLIF(sum(sum(duration_ms)) OVER (), 0) * 100.0)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS total_time_pct FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') GROUP BY step_name ORDER BY total_time_pct DESC NULLS LAST, p95_ms DESC;

panels.title: Run Duration vs Trades per Run
panels.description: Correlates runtime with trading activity. If duration increases while trades stay flat, overhead is growing in non-trading steps.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(r.started_at, $__interval), 'run_seconds' AS metric, avg(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (r.ended_at - r.started_at)))::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_runs r WHERE $__timeFilter(r.started_at) AND r.ended_at IS NOT NULL AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR r.bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${status}' = '__all' OR r.status = '${status}') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(r.started_at, $__interval), 'trades_per_run' AS metric, avg(COALESCE(t.trade_count, 0))::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_runs r LEFT JOIN ( SELECT run_id, count(*) AS trade_count FROM portal_bot_trades GROUP BY run_id ) t ON t.run_id = r.run_id WHERE $__timeFilter(r.started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR r.bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${status}' = '__all' OR r.status = '${status}') GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Internal Phase p95 (ms)
panels.description: This splits the main step into internal phases. The phase line that trends upward is your real slowdown source.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), step_name AS metric, percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND step_name IN ('step_signal_eval', 'step_decision_flow', 'step_trade_event_processing', 'step_finalize_bar', 'step_update_state', 'step_push_update') GROUP BY 1, step_name ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Internal Phase Breakdown (Selected Run)
panels.description: Average, p95, and total share by phase for one run. Focus first on phases with both high p95 and high total-time percentage.

targets.rawSql: WITH base AS ( SELECT step_name, duration_ms FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND step_name IN ('step_signal_eval', 'step_decision_flow', 'step_trade_event_processing', 'step_finalize_bar', 'step_update_state', 'step_push_update') ) SELECT step_name, count(*) AS calls, ROUND(avg(duration_ms)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS avg_ms, ROUND(percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS p95_ms, ROUND(sum(duration_ms)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS total_ms, ROUND((sum(duration_ms) / NULLIF(sum(sum(duration_ms)) OVER (), 0) * 100.0)::numeric, 2)::double precision AS total_time_pct FROM base GROUP BY step_name ORDER BY total_time_pct DESC NULLS LAST, p95_ms DESC;

panels.title: Push Payload Size p95 (KB)
panels.description: How large each bar update payload is. A rising payload size usually means serialized candles/trades are growing and driving CPU + network cost.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), percentile_cont(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY ((context->>'payload_bytes')::double precision / 1024.0))::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND step_name = 'step_push_update' AND (context->>'payload_bytes') IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

panels.title: Push Payload Object Counts
panels.description: Tracks how many candles, trades, logs, and decisions are included in each update. If these keep growing, step time drift is expected.

targets.rawSql: SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'candles' AS metric, avg((context->>'candles_count')::double precision)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND step_name = 'step_push_update' AND (context->>'candles_count') IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'trades' AS metric, avg((context->>'trades_count')::double precision)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND step_name = 'step_push_update' AND (context->>'trades_count') IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'logs' AS metric, avg((context->>'logs_count')::double precision)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND step_name = 'step_push_update' AND (context->>'logs_count') IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

SELECT $__timeGroupAlias(started_at, $__interval), 'decisions' AS metric, avg((context->>'decisions_count')::double precision)::double precision AS value FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) AND ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') AND ('${run_id}' = '__all' OR run_id = '${run_id}') AND step_name = 'step_push_update' AND (context->>'decisions_count') IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

list.name: bot_id
list.label: Bot
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT bot_id AS __value, bot_id AS __text FROM portal_bot_runs WHERE bot_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY 1;

list.name: run_id
list.label: Run
list.query: SELECT run_id AS __value, run_id AS __text FROM portal_bot_runs WHERE ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 500;

list.name: step_name
list.label: Step
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT step_name AS __value, step_name AS __text FROM portal_bot_run_steps WHERE ('${bot_id}' = '__all' OR bot_id = '${bot_id}') ORDER BY 1;
